heading: Repealed. ,Aug. 1, 1956, ch. 80770 Stat. 774
label: 176 to 181 Repealed. ,Aug. 1, 1956, ch. 80770 Stat. 774
notes: Act , repealed sections 176 to 181 effective upon the date which the President determined to be appropriate for the relinquishment of jurisdiction of the United States in Morocco. Jurisdiction of the United States in Morocco was relinquished by memorandum of President Eisenhower dated . Notice was given to Morocco on , and all pending cases were disposed of by 1960. See Bulletin of the State Department Vol. 35:909, page 844.Aug. 1, 1956Sept. 15, 1956Oct. 6, 1956
Section 176, R.S. § 4128, related to the exercise of judicial duties by the Secretary of State in the absence of a minister.

Section 177, R.S. §§ 4127, 4129; , , related to the general extension to unnamed countries with which the United States may after enter into treaty relations, of the provisions relating to the jurisdiction of consular and diplomatic officers.act June 14, 1878, ch. 19320 Stat. 131July 1, 1870
Section 178, R.S. § 4130; acts , ; , , related to the definition of the words “minister” and “consul”.Feb. 1, 1876, ch. 619 Stat. 2Feb. 5, 1915, ch. 23, § 638 Stat. 806
Section 179, R.S. § 4110, related to the responsibility of diplomatic and consular officers as judicial officers.
Section 180, R.S. § 4088; , , related to the power of consuls in uncivilized countries or countries not recognized by treaties.act Apr. 5, 1906, ch. 1366, § 334 Stat. 100
Section 181, R.S. § 4125, related to the applicability of other laws to Turkey.
